en: I came out and go, and I just, I was, I'm scared. I'm actually scared.
It was, it was so dangerous. They're not actors. They're untrained.
They're two seven-year-old, and they're brothers. They're twins. The purity of their vulnerability was the scary thing?
No, just the fact that they are- Mental and out of control. Yeah, no.
ko: 정신이 없고 통제할 수 없어. 그래, 아니야.

en: You know, they were fighting each other. They were throwing insults at me.
They were tearing around the room. Oh, wow.
Yeah, but they are extraordinary. They really are.
ko: 하지만 그래, 그들은 특별해. 정말 그래.

en: Richard, who's the slightly older one,
ko: 그리고 리처드, 조금 더 나이 많은 아이가,

en: by seconds to Henry, is sort of preternaturally old soul.
ko: 헨리보다 몇 초 더 나이가 많고, 마치 초자연적으로 노련한 영혼 같아.

en: He looks older, even though they are seconds apart in birth.
ko: 그들은 나이가 같음에도 불구하고 더 늙어 보이네.

en: And there were two moments that I remember very chiefly how we kind of got through it and can't quite believe we did it.
ko: 그리고 내가 기억하는 두 순간이 있었어.

en: One moment when I'm telling them, and their mothers died,
ko: 한 순간은 내가 그들에게 엄마가 돌아가셨다고 말할 때,

en: and Richard just found it unbelievably funny from the first moment.
ko: 리처드는 그걸 믿을 수 없을 만큼 웃겼어.

en: He just came towards me and went, poof! I'm not a critic.
And then, you know, eventually he got into it.
